pattern: Carrying a parallel stack of running minimums makes the minimum query O(1) instead of an O(n) scan.
hints: The minimum can only change when you push or pop, so you never need to search for it during a query.
Alongside the value stack, keep a second stack whose top is always the minimum of everything currently stored.
On push, store min(value, current overall min); on pop, discard both tops together; top() and getMin() just read the two tops.

edge_inputs.description: push descending values then pop back up, tracking the min
single element is both top and min
duplicate minimums must survive a single pop
min restores to an earlier value after popping the smaller one

stress.description: long randomized push/pop sequence, never popping an empty stack
stress.note: queries only issued when the stack is non-empty
